article.headline: Fire put 7 in double figures in 102-92 win over Dream, who play without injured Reese
article.description: — Megan Gustafson scored 17 points, two others had double-doubles and the Portland Fire held off the Atlanta Dream, who were without All-Star Angel Reese, 102-92 on Saturday.
article.linkText: Fire put 7 in double figures in 102-92 win over Dream, who play without injured Reese

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Atlanta Dream vs. Fire - Game Highlights) 2026/07/11 ESHEET

article.story: COLLEGE PARK, Ga. -- — Megan Gustafson scored 17 points, two others had double-doubles and the Portland Fire held off the Atlanta Dream, who were without All-Star Angel Reese, 102-92 on Saturday. The Fire (10-13) put seven players in double figures, with Emily Engstler getting 14 points and 11 rebounds and Carla Leite 13 points and 10 assists. Bridget Carleton had 12 points, hitting four of Portland's 14 3-pointers. Serah Williams scored 15 points off the bench, while Frieda Buhner had 11 and Teja Oblak 10. The three reserves combined to shoot 14 for 15. Portland was 14 of 29 from 3-point range and shot 60% (36 of 60) overall with 28 assists, seven from Oblak. Allisha Gray led the Dream (13-10) with 20 points. Madina Okot had a career-high 19 points and added eight rebounds starting for Reese, who was on the bench with her right foot in a boot from an injury in Thursday's win over Seattle. Naz Hillmon had 15 points, reserve Sika Kone added 11 and Jordin Canada had 10 points and 12 assists. Portland had an eight-point lead after three quarters and got the first double-figure lead at 91-81 on Oblak's 3 midway through the fourth quarter. Her floater made it 95-83 with 3 1/2 minutes to go. Portland was 5 for 5 inside and made 4 of 9 3s to take a 25-23 lead after one quarter. The Fire added five more 3s in the second quarter to push the lead to 51-44. Rhyne Howard hit two 3-pointers early in the second half and the Dream went up 70-67 with less than four minutes to go in the third quarter, but the Fire recovered and led 83-75 entering the fourth quarter. Up next Fire: At Connecticut on Tuesday. Dream: Host Los Angeles on Monday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball

articles.headline: Mystics power past short-handed Tempo 79-62 behind double-doubles by Iriafen and Austin
articles.description: — Kiki Iriafen had 25 points and 14 rebounds, Shakira Austin added 17 points and 10 rebounds, and the Washington Mystics beat the Toronto Tempo 79-62 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Paige Bueckers bemoans WNBA's lack of Black women head coaches
articles.description: Wings star Paige Bueckers called out the lack of Black women head coaches in the WNBA, saying Black women deserve "the same equal opportunity" to coach in the league.

images.name: Paige Bueckers [1296x729]
images.caption: Paige Bueckers is averaging 19.8 points and 5.8 assists but is day-to-day because of a right ankle injury and missed Saturday's loss at Portland.

articles.headline: Los Angeles visits Minnesota following McBride's 37-point game
articles.description: Los Angeles Sparks (10-12, 5-6 Western Conference) at Minnesota Lynx (18-6, 11-1 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Lynx rookie Miles reaches 400 points, 100 rebounds, 100 assists
articles.description: Minnesota rookie Olivia Miles scored a career-high 33, becoming the first WNBA player to reach 400 points, 100 rebounds and 100 assists in only 22 career games, and the Lynx defeated the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 Monday night for a third straight victory.

images.name: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury
images.caption: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury

articles.headline: Kayla McBride, Olivia Miles star in the Lynx's 104-100 win over the Mercury
articles.description: — Kayla McBride scored a season-high 37 points, rookie Olivia Miles had a career-high 33 and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 on Monday night for their third straight victory.
